content:
===center
##### High Power Squirrels!

Chatterfang can be a decent fringe cEDH contender, however I've opted to make it a hair more about tokens to play vs other not-quite-cEDH decks in our playgroup. The main goal is to combo out with [[Pitiless Plunderer]] or [[Warren Soultrader]] of course, but I'm also down to smash face with a Squirrel army if needed.

===endcenter

===center
***
🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿
***
<big><big><big><big><big><big><big><big>🐿 Emo Squirrel Squad 🐿</big></big></big></big></big></big></big></big>
***
🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿
***
===endcenter

===accordion
===panel: S Q U I R R E L
The deck seeks to play into a Grindy Midrange play style, which works quite well with the options provided by [[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]. It's not hard to grind out value, control what creatures stick around, and produce piles of Squirrels... And who doesn't love Squirrels? 🐿

===accordion
===panel: Why play this?
* You like to cast your Commander
* You enjoy Midrange decks
* You like Squirrels
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: Why NOT to play this
* You want your Commander to produce card advantage
* You hate Squirrels :(
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: GAMEPLAN

===accordion
===panel: Build Your Army, Sacrifice Squirrels, ?????, Profit
The general game plan is to ramp out [[Chatterfang, Squirrel General]] and a Token engine. Doing so not only grows your board to pressure opponents, but also allows you to leverage your Squirrels in order to keep Stax, Commanders, and creature based combos off the table. Holding up [[symbol:B]] also serves to deter removal on Chatterfang as you can sac itself in response as retaliation. Beyond just going wide, some engines, like [[Chitterspitter]], also help you go tall to overtake the board and your potential wincons, like [[Blood Artist]], heavily punish boardwipes.

When you find a moment where shields are down, it's time to search up [[Pitiless Plunderer]] / [[Warren Soultrader]] and go for the kill. These do also require an outlet to win, but there are tons of cards that can fill that role. If you don't have access to Plunderer/Soultrader or Chatterfang, there are a few back up combos the deck can fall back on.
===endpanel
===endaccordion  

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center
***
🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿
***
===endcenter

===accordion
===panel: COMBOS

===accordion
===panel: The Nuts
![Chatterfang](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/239727.jpg) ![Plunderer](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/155775.jpg)![Squirrel](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/228745.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: Chatterfang + Plunderer
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Chatterfang, Squirrel General]] + [[Pitiless Plunderer]]
- Mana: [[symbol:B]] to activate Chatterfang
- Other: 2 Squirrels (or 1 Squirrel + something of yours to kill)
***
**Sequencing**
1. If you have 1 Squirrel: Pay [[symbol:B]] + sacrifice your Squirrel to activate Chatterfang targetting your throwaway creature. Repeat until it dies so that Plunderer creates a 2nd Squirrel.
2. Pay [[symbol:B]] + sacrifice 2 Squirrels to activate Chatterfang targetting anything you wish to kill.
3. Plunderer will trigger and create 2 Treasures, which also creates 2 new Squirrels.
4. Before the Chatterfang ability resolves, crack a Treasure for [[symbol:B]].
5. Repeat steps 2 - 4 many times. You create an extra Treasure each loop.
6. Allow all the Chatterfang abilities to resolve.
***
**Tips & Tricks**
- If no one has a creature you can also target something of your own repeatedly, including Chatterfang itself.
- With a single Squirrel you can wipe the board, but do not gain Treasures.
***
**Result**
- You get infinite Treasures.
- You get infinite ETB and LTB of Treasures and Squirrels.
- You can kill any creatures on the board.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: Squirrel Wranglers
How to reach a win from Chatterfang + Plunderer
***
===accordion
===panel: Wincons
**[[Blood Artist]] / [[Zulaport Cutthroat]] / [[Marionette Apprentice]] / [[The Meathook Massacre]]**
- These result in infinite pings from sacrificing Squirrels.
- Artist/Zulaport/Marionette are able to be searched up from [[Eldritch Evolution]] on a Squirrel.
- Meathook doubles as a Boardwipe if needed.
***
**[[Acererak the Archlich]]**
- Repeatedly recast Acererak and Venture through [[ Lost Mine of Phandelver]] (and/or [[Dungeon of the Mad Mage]]).
- Doing so will create more Squirrels, ping Opponents, and draw cards.
- If you're short on cards and the pings don't kill them, drawing into another Wincon will.
***
**[[Finale of Devastation]]**
- Pump + Haste the team and go to beat street! Finale is an excellent Tutor/Reanimate that can incidentally win you the game as well.
- This does require enough creatures to swing in at each Opponent unblocked.
===endpanel
===endaccordion
===accordion
===panel: Other Outlets: Drawing Cards & Creating Squirrels
**[[Ruthless Knave]]**
- This will produce infinite Squirrels on top of drawing your deck.
- This can function as a serviceable Squirrel producer in the midgame provided you have the extra mana.
***
**[[Skullclamp]]**
- This will simply draw your deck.
- Skullclamp is a great card advantage engine if you have some Tokens to spare.
***
**[[Squirrel Sanctuary]]**
- Sanctuary will give you infinite Squirrels.
- Repeatedly sac Chatterfang to itself (target an opposing creature or a Squirrel) to get bounce triggers for Sanctuary. Recast Chatterfang followed by Sanctuary netting a Squirrel each time.
- This needs [[Finale of Devastation]] for Haste to directly translate into a win. 
***
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: A Treasure For Your Soul
![Chatterfang](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/239727.jpg) ![Soultrader](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/552652.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: Chatterfang + Soultrader
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Chatterfang, Squirrel General]] + [[Warren Soultrader]]
- Mana: N/A
- Other: A creature to sacrifice to Soultrader.  An Outlet needs to be available for this to result in a win.
***
**Sequencing**
1. Sacrifice your creature to activate Soultrader. You lose 1 Life and get both a Treasure and Squirrel. 
2. Repeat as needed sacing your new Squirrel to net a Treasure each loop.
3. Cast, Tutor, or Reanimate your Outlet and win.
***
**Tips & Tricks**
- While this does require a 3rd piece to actually win, that 3rd piece is pretty flexible and this combo gives you Treasures in order to find/Cast it.
***
**Result**
- You get as many Treasures as you want, only limited by your Life total.
- You get "infinite" ETB and LTB of Treasures and Squirrels.
- Artist/Zulaport serve as the best Outlets since you can combo off no matter current Life totals. Marrionette can work if you have enough Life to spend, while Meathook requires you to have the highest Life.
- A large Finale also works if you have enough Creatures out and enough Life to spend.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: AlurenLich
![Aluren](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/5455.jpg)![Archlich](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/243472.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: Aluren + Archlich
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Aluren]] + [[Acererak the Archlich]]
- Mana: [[symbol:2]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:G]] to cast Aluren
***
**Sequencing**
1. Pay [[symbol:2]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:G]] to cast Aluren.
2. Cast Acererak for free and Venture into [[Lost Mine of Phandelver]] (and/or [[Dungeon of the Mad Mage]]).
3. Scry, make a Treasure, make a 1/1 token(s), ping Opponents, or draw a card(s).
4. Return Acerarak to your hand.
5. Repeat steps 2 - 4.
***
**Tips & Tricks**
- Aluren can be dangerous and give the game to an Opponent. ONLY cast it when you have Acererak and are ready to combo off.
- This combo does not require access to Chatterfang to get going.
***
**Result**
- Ultimately you will ping Opponents to death or draw into a Wincon.
- Along the way you make Treasures to cast anything you draw as well as get some 1/1 tokens.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: Pirate Combo 2: Electric Boogaloo
![Chatterfang](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/239727.jpg)![Knave](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/145606.jpg)![Earthcraft](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/5529.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: Chatterfang + Knave + Earthcraft
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Chatterfang, Squirrel General]] + [[Ruthless Knave]] + [[Earthcraft]]
- Mana: [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] to activate Knave
- Other: A creature to sacrifice and a Basic Land
***
**Sequencing**
1. Pay [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] and sacrifice a creature to activate Knave. This produces 2 Treasures and 2 Squirrels.
2. Activate Earthcraft with both new Squirrels to untap a Basic twice and float [[symbol:BG]][[symbol:BG]].
3. Use the floating [[symbol:BG]][[symbol:BG]] + crack a Treasure to repeat steps 1 - 2. This nets a Treasure each loop.
4. Activate Knave's 2nd ability by sacrificing 3 Treasures to draw a card. Repeat as needed.
***
**Tips & Tricks**
- If you need untapped Squirrels for [[Finale of Devastation]]: repeat the above loop, but use excess treasures instead of tapping Squirrels to pay for Knaves 1st ability.
***
**Result**
- You get infinite Treasures.
- You get infinite Squirrels (some tapped, some untapped).
- You draw as many cards as you want.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: Saw The Weak Witness
![EWit](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/179485.jpg)![Saw](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/257009.jpg)![Culling](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/4307.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: EWit + Saw + Culling
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Eternal Witness]] + [[Saw in Half]] + [[Culling the Weak]]
- Mana: [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:B]] to cast Saw and Culling
- Other: EWit on the battlefield. An Outlet needs to be available for this to result in a win.
***
**Sequencing**
1. Pay [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] to cast Saw destroying EWit. This produces 2 EWit Tokens.
2. Use the EWit triggers to add back Saw in Half and any other card you want (such as an Outlet for this combo).
3. Pay [[symbol:B]] to cast Culling destroying an EWit Token and adding [[symbol:B]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:B]].
4. Pay [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] to cast Saw destroying an EWit Token. This produces 2 new EWit Tokens.
5. Use the EWit triggers to add back both Saw in Half and Culling the Weak.
6. Repeat Steps 3-5 as much as you want creating infinite ETB, LTB, and death triggers of Creatures.
 ***
**Tips & Tricks**
- This doesn't require Chatterfang and can be done at Instant Speed.
- Your original cast of EWit can recur back Saw or Culling if it happens to be in your Grave already.
- Culling can provide you with the required Mana to start if you have something else to sacrifice for it prior to casting Saw.
- Culling can start out in your Grave, but you miss out on adding back any other card once. EWit being out and casting Saw is all you need in that case.
- Reanimating/Convoking out [[Hoarding Broodlord]] can also kickstart this combo by tutoring up Saw for itself with the token copies then getting EWit + Culling. This does require extra bodies for Convoke or extra Mana (Chatterfang being out greatly helps with the Convoke costs). End the loop by Sawing Broodlord once more to tutor up your Outlet.
***
**Result**
- You get infinite ETB, LTB, and Death triggers from EWit Tokens.
- Artist/Zulaport/Marionette/Meathook are a direct win alongside this loop.
- Other Outlets include: Plunderer/Earthcraft for Mana, and Chatterfang for Squirrels.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===accordion
===panel: The Witness Saw Squirrels
![Chatterfang](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/239727.jpg)![EWit](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/179485.jpg)![Saw](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/257009.jpg)![Earthcraft](https://product-images.tcgplayer.com/fit-in/437x437/5529.jpg)
***
===accordion
===panel: Chatterfang + EWit + Saw + Earthcraft
**Requirements**
- Cards: [[Chatterfang, Squirrel General]] + [[Eternal Witness]] + [[Saw in Half]] + [[Earthcraft]]
- Mana: [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] to cast Saw
- Other: A Basic Swamp, preferably also a Basic Forest
***
**Sequencing**
1. Pay [[symbol:2]][[symbol:B]] to cast Saw destroying EWit. This produces 2 EWit Tokens and 2 Squirrels.
2. Use the EWit triggers to add back Saw in Half and any other card you want.
3. Activate Earthcraft with 3 of the new Tokens to untap a Basic 3 times and float [[symbol:B]][[symbol:BG]][[symbol:BG]].
3. Use the floating [[symbol:B]][[symbol:BG]][[symbol:BG]] to repeat steps 1 - 3. This nets an untapped Token and gets back an additional card of your choice each loop.
4. Use the untapped Tokens to create Infinite Mana off your Basic(s) and loop any other cards you want since the EWit Tokens keep adding things back to hand.
***
**Tips & Tricks**
- This is definitely a back up attempt at winning rather than something to actively aim for, however it's still nice to have and worth pointing out.
***
**Result**
- You get infinite Mana, but only what your Basics can produce.
- You get infinite Squirrels and EWit Tokens (some tapped, some untapped).
- You recast any cards in your Grave as many times as you want.
===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion


===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center
***
🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿[[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]][[symbol:B]][[symbol:G]]🐿
***
===endcenter
